diagrams.title: Agile Manifesto Values Pyramid
diagrams.description: Hierarchical representation showing how agile values relate to principles and practices
elements: Top Level: VALUES (What we believe) - Individuals/interactions, Working software, Customer collaboration, Responding to change
Middle Level: PRINCIPLES (Why we believe) - 12 principles behind the manifesto
Base Level: PRACTICES (What we do) - Specific techniques like daily standups, sprints, retrospectives
diagrams.purpose: Shows that values guide principles which guide practices, and values take precedence when conflicts arise

diagrams.title: Cost of Change Curve Comparison
diagrams.description: Graph comparing cost of changes in traditional vs agile approaches
elements: X-axis: Project Timeline (Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Maintenance)
Traditional Curve: Exponential rise - low early, very high late
Agile Curve: Relatively flat - changes remain low throughout due to iterative feedback
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates why agility is preferred - changes cost less throughout development because they are incorporated early and continuously validated
